mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5% and remains elevated for 2+ months, equity risk premiums expand as the real discount rate rises, compressing present values of future corporate earnings. This produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with restrictive policy rates
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 4.5% for 2+ months, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month decline in total nonfarm payroll employment signals labor market deterioration and rising recession risk, triggering equity selloffs as investors reprice growth expectations downward. This produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 50% of months following PAYEMS declines, or correlation between PAYEMS momentum and 60-day SPX returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) combined with stable or declining unemployment (UNRATE) indicates robust consumer demand and tight labor markets, signaling sustained economic expansion. This dual confirmation produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as earnings growth expectations rise.
regime_hint: mid-cycle expansion with strong consumer activity
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 45% of triggered instances, or the joint signal (RSAFS growth AND low UNRATE) shows Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines month-over-month while inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains elevated, indicating stagflationary pressure. This combination signals margin compression for corporates and reduced real growth, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as investors immediately reprice risk.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month decline > 0.3% AND CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth > 3.0%
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or the interaction signal shows Rank IC < 0.03 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) decline by more than 10% month-over-month, signaling a sharp contraction in residential investment and forward-looking demand weakness. This leading indicator produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as the market reprices growth expectations.
regime_hint: demand-side slowdown with housing sector weakness
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of instances following > 10% HOUST declines, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When unemployment (UNRATE) rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month period while payroll employment (PAYEMS) stagnates, labor market deterioration signals imminent recession. This dual deterioration produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in 3 months AND PAYEMS month-over-month growth < 0.1%
regime_hint: labor market deterioration phase preceding recession
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or the joint signal shows Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) accelerates quarter-over-quarter while the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) remains below 3.5%, indicating accommodative monetary policy supporting expansion. This regime produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as earnings growth accelerates.
regime_hint: accommodative monetary policy supporting economic expansion
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 45% of triggered instances, or the interaction signal shows Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) accelerates month-over-month by more than 0.5% while inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains stable year-over-year below 2.5%, indicating healthy supply-side growth without inflationary pressure. This produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as investors reward productivity gains.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month growth > 0.5% AND CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth < 2.5%
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or the interaction signal shows Rank IC < 0.03 over any 1-year rolling window.
